title: iOS Subscription App Stack
short_description: Production-ready toolchain for building an iOS app with in-app purchases, subscriptions, crash monitoring, and growth analytics.
long_description: Launching a paid iOS app in 2026 means wrestling with StoreKit 2, App Store Review, receipt validation, subscription lifecycle management, churn prevention, and attribution — all before a single user pays you. This stack wires together the best-in-class tools for each concern so you can ship a monetised iOS app without building payment plumbing from scratch. RevenueCat handles the entire subscription lifecycle (purchase, restore, grace periods, billing-retry), Superwall lets you A/B test paywalls without resubmitting to App Store Review, and AppsFlyer ties every install to the campaign that drove it — all while Sentry and Firebase Crashlytics keep the app stable. The result is a repeatable, revenue-optimised iOS monetisation architecture that works at every scale, from indie launch to Series B.
target_audience: iOS developers, indie hackers, and mobile growth teams shipping subscription-based apps on the App Store

tools.why: Abstracts StoreKit 2 and StoreKit 1, handles receipt validation, webhooks, entitlements, and subscriber lifecycle events across all Apple platforms with a single SDK.
Lets you iterate on paywall copy, pricing, and layout remotely without an App Store release cycle, with built-in conversion tracking.
Firebase Auth handles Sign in with Apple (required by App Store guidelines), Firestore stores user data, and Remote Config controls feature flags without a redeploy.
SKAdNetwork-compatible MMP that accurately attributes paid installs to campaigns and passes conversion values back to ad networks under iOS 14+ privacy rules.
Captures Swift/Objective-C crashes and ANRs with full stack traces, session replay, and performance breadcrumbs — faster triage than native Xcode organiser.
Google's free crash reporter integrates natively with Firebase Analytics for crash-free users metric and is required by many App Store Connect data dashboards.
Tracks funnel steps from install → trial → paid → churn with cohort retention analysis, crucial for validating paywall experiments driven by Superwall.
Sends trial-ending reminders, re-engagement nudges, and feature announcements via APNs with a visual automation builder — no backend code required.
Automates code signing, screenshot generation, and App Store Connect uploads so every release is a single command rather than a 45-minute manual process.

gotchas: StoreKit 2 (iOS 15+) uses async/await and server-to-server JWS receipts instead of the legacy base64 receipt blob. If you support iOS 14, RevenueCat's SDK silently falls back to StoreKit 1 — but your backend webhooks will look different. Audit your webhook handlers for both receipt formats before launch.

SKAdNetwork 4.0 gives you up to 64 conversion values and two postback windows, but ad networks must be registered in your Info.plist. A missing or stale SKAdNetworkItems list means zero attribution for entire ad networks. Run AppsFlyer's SKAdNetwork validation tool before your first paid campaign.

Apple's App Store Review guideline 3.1.1 requires Sign in with Apple whenever any other third-party social login is offered. Firebase Auth supports Sign in with Apple, but you must handle the 'real name' and private relay email edge cases (users may get a randomised @privaterelay.appleid.com address) — store the Apple User ID as your canonical identifier, not the email.
estimated_monthly_cost: $0–$450 at launch (RevenueCat free up to $2.5k MRR, Firebase Spark free tier, AppsFlyer free up to 6k installs/month, Amplitude free up to 10M events, OneSignal free up to 10k subscribers). Scales to ~$500–$2k/month at $50k MRR.

seo.title: iOS Subscription App Stack — RevenueCat, Superwall, AppsFlyer & More
seo.meta_description: The definitive tool stack for building a monetised iOS subscription app in 2026. Covers StoreKit 2, paywall A/B testing, SKAdNetwork attribution, crash monitoring, and push notifications.
